And he took them<S>3947</S>, and sent them over<S>5674</S> the brook<S>5158</S>, and sent over<S>5674</S> that he had. <sup>sent them: Heb. caused to pass</sup>

37.text: And Jacob<S>3290</S> dwelt<S>3427</S> in the land<S>776</S> wherein his father<S>1</S> was a stranger<S>4033</S>, in the land<S>776</S> of Canaan<S>3667</S>. <sup>wherein: Heb. of his father's sojournings</sup>

And he told<S>5608</S> it to his father<S>1</S>, and to his brethren<S>251</S>: and his father<S>1</S> rebuked<S>1605</S> him, and said<S>559</S> unto him, What is this dream<S>2472</S> that thou hast dreamed<S>2492</S>? And he said<S>559</S> to him, Go<S>3212</S>, I pray thee, see<S>7200</S> whether it be well<S>7965</S> with thy brethren<S>251</S>, and well<S>7965</S> with the flocks<S>6629</S>; and bring<S>7725</S> me word<S>1697</S> again<S>7725</S>. And a certain man<S>376</S> found him<S>4672</S>, and, behold, he was wandering<S>8582</S> in the field<S>7704</S>: and the man<S>376</S> asked him<S>7592</S>, saying<S>559</S>, What seekest<S>1245</S> thou?
And he said<S>559</S>, I seek<S>1245</S> my brethren<S>251</S>: tell<S>5046</S> me, I pray thee, where<S>375</S> they feed<S>7462</S> their flocks.

And the man<S>376</S> said<S>559</S>, They are departed<S>5265</S> hence<S>2088</S>; for I heard<S>8085</S> them say<S>559</S>, Let us go<S>3212</S> to Dothan<S>1886</S>. And Joseph<S>3130</S> went<S>3212</S> after<S>310</S> his brethren<S>251</S>, and found<S>4672</S> them in Dothan<S>1886</S>.
And when they saw<S>7200</S> him afar off<S>7350</S>, even before he came near<S>7126</S> unto them, they conspired<S>5230</S> against him to slay<S>4191</S> him.
And they said<S>559</S> one<S>376</S> to another<S>251</S>, Behold, this<S>1976</S> dreamer<S>1167</S> <S>2472</S> cometh<S>935</S>. <sup>dreamer: Heb. master of dreams</sup>

Come<S>3212</S> now therefore, and let us slay<S>2026</S> him, and cast<S>7993</S> him into some<S>259</S> pit<S>953</S>, and we will say<S>559</S>, Some evil<S>7451</S> beast<S>2416</S> hath devoured<S>398</S> him: and we shall see<S>7200</S> what will become of his dreams<S>2472</S>.

And Reuben<S>7205</S> said<S>559</S> unto them, Shed<S>8210</S> no blood<S>1818</S>, but cast<S>7993</S> him into this pit<S>953</S> that is in the wilderness<S>4057</S>, and lay<S>7971</S> no hand<S>3027</S> upon him; that he might rid<S>5337</S> him out of their hands<S>3027</S>, to deliver<S>7725</S> him to his father<S>1</S> again<S>7725</S>.

And they sat down<S>3427</S> to eat<S>398</S> bread<S>3899</S>: and they lifted up<S>5375</S> their eyes<S>5869</S> and looked<S>7200</S>, and, behold, a company<S>736</S> of Ishmeelites<S>3459</S> came<S>935</S> from Gilead<S>1568</S> with their camels<S>1581</S> bearing<S>5375</S> spicery<S>5219</S> and balm<S>6875</S> and myrrh<S>3910</S>, going<S>1980</S> to carry it down<S>3381</S> to Egypt<S>4714</S>.

Come<S>3212</S>, and let us sell<S>4376</S> him to the Ishmeelites<S>3459</S>, and let not our hand<S>3027</S> be upon him; for he is our brother<S>251</S> and our flesh<S>1320</S>. And his brethren<S>251</S> were content<S>8085</S>. <sup>were: Heb. hearkened</sup>

Then there passed<S>5674</S> by Midianites<S>4084</S> merchantmen<S>582</S> <S>5503</S>; and they drew<S>4900</S> and lifted up<S>5927</S> Joseph<S>3130</S> out of the pit<S>953</S>, and sold<S>4376</S> Joseph<S>3130</S> to the Ishmeelites<S>3459</S> for twenty<S>6242</S> pieces of silver<S>3701</S>: and they brought<S>935</S> Joseph<S>3130</S> into Egypt<S>4714</S>.

And they sent<S>7971</S> the coat<S>3801</S> of many colours<S>6446</S>, and they brought<S>935</S> it to their father<S>1</S>; and said<S>559</S>, This have we found<S>4672</S>: know<S>5234</S> now whether it be thy son's<S>1121</S> coat<S>3801</S> or no.

And Jacob<S>3290</S> rent<S>7167</S> his clothes<S>8071</S>, and put<S>7760</S> sackcloth<S>8242</S> upon his loins<S>4975</S>, and mourned<S>56</S> for his son<S>1121</S> many<S>7227</S> days<S>3117</S>.

And the Midianites<S>4092</S> sold<S>4376</S> him into Egypt<S>4714</S> unto Potiphar<S>6318</S>, an officer<S>5631</S> of Pharaoh's<S>6547</S>, and captain<S>8269</S> of the guard<S>2876</S>. <sup>officer: Heb. eunuch: but the word doth signify not only eunuchs, but also chamberlains, courtiers, and officers</sup> <sup>captain: or, chief marshal: Heb. chief of the slaughter men, or executioners</sup>

38.text: And it came to pass at that time<S>6256</S>, that Judah<S>3063</S> went down<S>3381</S> from his brethren<S>251</S>, and turned<S>5186</S> in to a certain<S>376</S> Adullamite<S>5726</S>, whose name<S>8034</S> was Hirah<S>2437</S>.

And Er<S>6147</S>, Judah's<S>3063</S> firstborn<S>1060</S>, was wicked<S>7451</S> in the sight<S>5869</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>; and the LORD<S>3068</S> slew<S>4191</S> him.

And Judah<S>3063</S> said<S>559</S> unto Onan<S>209</S>, Go in<S>935</S> unto thy brother's<S>251</S> wife<S>802</S>, and marry<S>2992</S> her, and raise up<S>6965</S> seed<S>2233</S> to thy brother<S>251</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> Judah<S>3063</S> to Tamar<S>8559</S> his daughter in law<S>3618</S>, Remain<S>3427</S> a widow<S>490</S> at thy father's<S>1</S> house<S>1004</S>, till Shelah<S>7956</S> my son<S>1121</S> be grown<S>1431</S>: for he said<S>559</S>, Lest peradventure he die<S>4191</S> also, as his brethren<S>251</S> did. And he turned<S>5186</S> unto her by the way<S>1870</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Go to<S>3051</S>, I pray thee, let me come in<S>935</S> unto thee; (for he knew<S>3045</S> not that she was his daughter in law<S>3618</S>.) Then he asked<S>7592</S> the men<S>582</S> of that place<S>4725</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Where is the harlot<S>6948</S>, that was openly<S>5869</S> by the way side<S>1870</S>? And they said<S>559</S>, There was no harlot<S>6948</S> in this<S>2088</S> place. <sup>openly: or, in Enajim</sup>

And he returned<S>7725</S> to Judah<S>3063</S>, and said<S>559</S>, I cannot<S>3808</S> find<S>4672</S> her; and also the men<S>582</S> of the place<S>4725</S> said<S>559</S>, that there was no harlot<S>6948</S> in this place.

And it came to pass about three<S>7969</S> months<S>2320</S> after, that it was told<S>5046</S> Judah<S>3063</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Tamar<S>8559</S> thy daughter in law<S>3618</S> hath played the harlot<S>2181</S>; and also, behold, she is with child<S>2030</S> by whoredom<S>2183</S>. When she was brought forth<S>3318</S>, she sent<S>7971</S> to her father in law<S>2524</S>, saying<S>559</S>, By the man<S>376</S>, whose these are, am I with child<S>2030</S>: and she said<S>559</S>, Discern<S>5234</S>, I pray thee, whose are these, the signet<S>2858</S>, and bracelets<S>6616</S>, and staff<S>4294</S>.

And their father<S>1</S> Israel<S>3478</S> said<S>559</S> unto them, If it must be so now<S>645</S>, do<S>6213</S> this; take<S>3947</S> of the best fruits<S>2173</S> in the land<S>776</S> in your vessels<S>3627</S>, and carry down<S>3381</S> the man<S>376</S> a present<S>4503</S>, a little<S>4592</S> balm<S>6875</S>, and a little<S>4592</S> honey<S>1706</S>, spices<S>5219</S>, and myrrh<S>3910</S>, nuts<S>992</S>, and almonds<S>8247</S>:

And take<S>3947</S> double<S>4932</S> money<S>3701</S> in your hand<S>3027</S>; and the money<S>3701</S> that was brought again<S>7725</S> in the mouth<S>6310</S> of your sacks<S>572</S>, carry it again<S>7725</S> in your hand<S>3027</S>; peradventure it was an oversight<S>4870</S>:
Take<S>3947</S> also your brother<S>251</S>, and arise<S>6965</S>, go again<S>7725</S> unto the man<S>376</S>:

And the men<S>582</S> took<S>3947</S> that<S>2063</S> present<S>4503</S>, and they took<S>3947</S> double<S>4932</S> money<S>3701</S> in their hand<S>3027</S>, and Benjamin<S>1144</S>; and rose up<S>6965</S>, and went down<S>3381</S> to Egypt<S>4714</S>, and stood<S>5975</S> before<S>6440</S> Joseph<S>3130</S>.

And the men<S>582</S> were afraid<S>3372</S>, because they were brought<S>935</S> into Joseph's<S>3130</S> house<S>1004</S>; and they said<S>559</S>, Because<S>1697</S> of the money<S>3701</S> that was returned<S>7725</S> in our sacks<S>572</S> at the first time<S>8462</S> are we brought in<S>935</S>; that he may seek occasion<S>1556</S> against us, and fall<S>5307</S> upon us, and take<S>3947</S> us for bondmen<S>5650</S>, and our asses<S>2543</S>. <sup>seek: Heb. roll himself upon us</sup>
And they came near<S>5066</S> to the steward<S>376</S> of Joseph's<S>3130</S> house<S>1004</S>, and they communed<S>1696</S> with him at the door<S>6607</S> of the house<S>1004</S>,
And said<S>559</S>, O<S>994</S> sir<S>113</S>, we came indeed<S>3381</S> down<S>3381</S> at the first time<S>8462</S> to buy<S>7666</S> food<S>400</S>: <sup>we: Heb. coming down we came down</sup>

And he said<S>559</S>, Peace<S>7965</S> be to you, fear<S>3372</S> not: your God<S>430</S>, and the God<S>430</S> of your father<S>1</S>, hath given<S>5414</S> you treasure<S>4301</S> in your sacks<S>572</S>: I had<S>935</S> your money<S>3701</S>. And Joseph<S>3130</S> made haste<S>4116</S>; for his bowels<S>7356</S> did yearn<S>3648</S> upon his brother<S>251</S>: and he sought<S>1245</S> where to weep<S>1058</S>; and he entered<S>935</S> into his chamber<S>2315</S>, and wept<S>1058</S> there.

45.text: Then Joseph<S>3130</S> could<S>3201</S> not refrain<S>662</S> himself before all them that stood<S>5324</S> by him; and he cried<S>7121</S>, Cause<S>3318</S> every man<S>376</S> to go out<S>3318</S> from me.
